claim: A novel Indium Gallium Nitride (InGaN) film has been used to form a mid-wave infrared (MWIR) sensor.
A novel 2-dimensional Indium Gallium Nitride (InGaN) film has been used to form a mid-wave infrared (MWIR) sensor.
A novel Indium Gallium Nitride (InGaN) film has been used to form a near-infrared (NIR) sensor.
The addition of a superconducting coil can help recover magnetic energy otherwise lost in a normal conducting railgun.
